title: Kube-OVN Pod Network Unreachable / CNI Failure
symptoms: Pods stuck in ContainerCreating with 'network: plugin type="kube-ovn" failed'
New pods get no IP address
kubectl describe pod shows 'failed to create pod sandbox: rpc error'
causes: kube-ovn-cni daemonset pod not running on the node
OVS (Open vSwitch) daemon not running on node
investigation: kubectl -n kube-system get pods -l app=kube-ovn-cni -o wide
remediation: Restart kube-ovn-cni daemonset: kubectl -n kube-system rollout restart ds/kube-ovn-cni
Restart OVS daemonset: kubectl -n kube-system rollout restart ds/ovs-ovn
Restart controller: kubectl -n kube-system rollout restart deploy/kube-ovn-controller
If CNI binary missing: delete cni pod on that node to force reinstall
For OVN DB issues: kubectl -n kube-system rollout restart deploy/ovn-central
Check node OVS: SSH to node, 'systemctl status ovs-vswitchd' — restart if needed
escalation: CNI failure on a node prevents all new pod scheduling on that node. Multiple nodes affected is a cluster-wide network P1.

title: Kube-OVN Subnet / IP Allocation Failure
symptoms: Pods getting duplicate IPs or wrong subnet IPs
kubectl get subnet shows subnet in NotReady state
New namespace pods cannot communicate with other namespaces
causes: Subnet CIDR overlap with existing subnet or node network
kube-ovn-controller failed to sync subnet to OVN
Subnet CIDRs conflict with MetalLB pool or cluster service CIDR
investigation: kubectl -n kube-system logs deploy/kube-ovn-controller | grep -i 'subnet\|error' | tail -50
kubectl get pod -A -o wide | awk '{print $7}' | sort | uniq -d  # find duplicate IPs
remediation: For subnet NotReady: kubectl -n kube-system rollout restart deploy/kube-ovn-controller
For IP exhaustion: expand subnet CIDR or create new subnet for overflow namespaces
For CIDR conflicts: coordinate with network team to redesign subnet layout
Trigger resync: kubectl -n kube-system exec deploy/kube-ovn-controller -- /kube-ovn/kube-ovn-controller --logtostderr --v=4
For duplicate IPs: delete the affected pod — controller will re-assign unique IP on restart
escalation: Subnet failures cause namespace-level network isolation failures. Escalate to network team if CIDR redesign needed.

title: Kube-OVN OVN Controller / Northbound DB Down
symptoms: kubectl -n kube-system get pods shows ovn-central in CrashLoopBackOff
kube-ovn-controller logs show 'failed to connect to OVN NB DB'
causes: RAFT election failure in OVN DB cluster (if HA mode)
Disk full on node running ovn-central (OVN DB uses disk)
remediation: Restart ovn-central: kubectl -n kube-system delete pod -l app=ovn-central
If disk full: clean up old logs on the node, expand PVC if persistent storage used
For RAFT split-brain: delete all ovn-central pods simultaneously to force re-election
Verify recovery: kubectl -n kube-system exec <ovn-central-pod> -- ovn-nbctl show
escalation: OVN DB outage causes complete network control plane failure. Existing connections continue but no new network policy changes take effect.

title: Kube-OVN Security Group / Network Policy Not Enforced
symptoms: NetworkPolicy objects exist but not taking effect
Kube-OVN SecurityGroup CR in error state
Pods can reach services they should be blocked from
causes: kube-ovn-controller not syncing ACLs to OVN
SecurityGroup CRD not supported by installed Kube-OVN version
investigation: kubectl -n kube-system logs deploy/kube-ovn-controller | grep -i 'acl\|security' | tail -50
remediation: Force ACL resync: kubectl -n kube-system rollout restart deploy/kube-ovn-controller
Verify OVN ACLs directly: exec into ovn-central pod, run ovn-nbctl acl-list
Check Kube-OVN version supports SecurityGroup feature
escalation: Incorrect network policy enforcement is a security issue. Escalate to security team if sensitive workloads are exposed.
